title: Agent observability: dashboard command with live TUI
description: Port overstory's rich observability to foreman. Add a 'foreman dashboard' command showing live agent status, current phase, token usage, cost tracking, and recent events in a TUI. Overstory has: dashboard, inspect, trace, replay, feed, logs, costs, metrics. Start with a unified dashboard view combining status + costs + phase progress.

title: Inter-agent messaging system (SQLite mail)
description: Implement an inter-agent messaging system similar to overstory's SQLite mail. Currently foreman agents communicate only via report files (EXPLORER_REPORT.md, QA_REPORT.md, REVIEW.md). A mail system would enable: real-time status updates between phases, escalation messages from stuck agents, nudge mechanism for stalled workers, broadcast messaging for coordination. Use SQLite WAL mode for concurrent access.

title: 4-tier merge conflict resolution
description: Upgrade foreman's merge system from basic merge + theirs strategy to overstory's 4-tier conflict resolution: 1) textual auto-merge, 2) AI-powered conflict resolution, 3) structured human review with context, 4) manual fallback. Currently foreman's refinery only does basic merge or -X theirs. The AI resolver tier would use Claude to understand both sides of a conflict and produce an intelligent merge.

title: Health monitoring: doctor command with auto-fix
description: Add a 'foreman doctor' command inspired by overstory's 11-category health check system. Check for: stale worktrees, orphaned branches, stuck agents, SQLite integrity, seeds health, git remote connectivity, disk space, process zombies, env var issues (CLAUDECODE leak), sdk version compatibility. Support --fix flag for auto-repair of common issues. Currently foreman only has basic stuck detection in monitor.

title: Multi-runtime support (pluggable AgentRuntime interface)
description: Abstract the Claude SDK dependency behind a pluggable AgentRuntime interface, similar to overstory's 8-runtime adapter system. Start with a RuntimeAdapter interface that wraps query() and result handling. Implement Claude SDK adapter first (extract from current agent-worker.ts), then add support for at least one additional runtime (e.g., Pi or Gemini CLI). This enables runtime-agnostic agent spawning.

title: Tool enforcement guards for agent roles
description: Implement mechanical tool enforcement for agent roles instead of relying on prompt instructions alone. Explorer and Reviewer should be physically prevented from writing files (not just told not to). Overstory uses runtime-specific guards: Claude Code settings.local.json hooks, file system permission restrictions. For foreman, generate per-worktree .claude/settings.local.json with role-appropriate permissions (read-only for explorer/reviewer, read-write for developer/qa).

title: Agent checkpoint save/restore for crash recovery
description: Add checkpoint save/restore so agents can survive crashes and context compaction. Overstory saves agent state snapshots that can be restored after failures. For foreman: save pipeline progress (which phases completed, report file contents, git diff state) to SQLite or JSON. On retry, restore checkpoint so the developer doesn't re-explore and the QA doesn't re-run passing tests. Currently foreman only resets the seed to open on failure.

title: Task groups for batch coordination
description: Implement task groups that auto-close when all member tasks complete, inspired by overstory's group system. Use case: decompose creates an epic with 10 tasks. A task group tracks the batch and auto-closes the epic when all children finish. Commands: 'foreman group create', 'foreman group status', 'foreman group add'. This is different from seeds parent-child deps — groups are coordination primitives, not dependency constraints.

title: Cost tracking with per-agent and per-phase breakdowns
description: Enhance foreman's cost tracking to match overstory's detailed breakdowns. Currently foreman tracks total cost and tokens per run. Add: per-phase cost breakdown (explorer vs developer vs QA vs reviewer), per-model cost tracking (haiku vs sonnet), cumulative project cost history, cost anomaly detection (flag runs that cost 3x+ average), 'foreman costs' command with --by-phase, --by-model, --by-run flags.

title: Gateway provider routing for model selection
description: Add gateway provider support for routing API calls through custom endpoints (z.ai, OpenRouter, self-hosted proxies). Overstory supports per-agent model routing with provider/model-id format. For foreman: allow configuring model providers in foreman config, route different pipeline phases through different providers (e.g., haiku via direct API, sonnet via z.ai), support provider health checking.

title: test-delete-me
Replace maxTurns with maxBudgetUsd for pipeline phase limits
description: Fixed maxTurns is fragile — explorer hit 30 turns in monorepo and failed. Switch to budget-based limits (maxBudgetUsd) per phase as primary limiter, with maxTurns as generous backstop. SDK already supports maxBudgetUsd natively. Add maxBudgetUsd to RoleConfig, pass to SDK query(), fall back to maxTurns when budget not set.

title: Add 'foreman seed' command for natural-language issue creation
description: New CLI command: foreman seed <description>. Takes a free-form description, uses LLM to analyze and categorize (feature/bug/task/epic/chore), set appropriate priority, and optionally decompose into subtasks. Workflow: parse input → LLM classification → sd create with correct type/priority → optional decomposition into child tasks with dependencies. Should feel like talking to a PM — just describe what you need and foreman figures out the rest.

title: Extract pipeline tuning constants to environment variables
description: Hardcoded values in the pipeline should be configurable via env vars for tuning without code changes. Primary target: maxBudgetUsd per phase (once foreman-fe78 lands). Also extract: MAX_DEV_RETRIES (currently 2), progress flush interval (currently 2000ms), finalize/sd command timeouts (30s/10s), sd maxBuffer (10MB). Use FOREMAN_ prefix, e.g. FOREMAN_MAX_DEV_RETRIES=2. Fall back to current hardcoded defaults when not set.

title: Extract per-phase model selection to environment variables
description: Model choices are hardcoded in roles.ts: explorer=haiku, developer/qa/reviewer=sonnet. These should be configurable via env vars (e.g. FOREMAN_EXPLORER_MODEL, FOREMAN_DEVELOPER_MODEL) so users can experiment with different models per phase without code changes. Fall back to current defaults.

title: Extract dispatcher maxTurns and model to configuration
description: dispatcher.ts has hardcoded maxTurns=50 and model=claude-sonnet-4-6 for single-agent (non-pipeline) mode. These should be configurable via env vars (FOREMAN_WORKER_MAX_TURNS, FOREMAN_WORKER_MODEL) for tuning.

title: Extract per-phase maxTurns to environment variables
description: roles.ts has hardcoded maxTurns per phase (explorer=30, developer=80, qa=30, reviewer=20). Once maxBudgetUsd is primary limiter (foreman-fe78), these become backstops but should still be tunable via env vars (e.g. FOREMAN_EXPLORER_MAX_TURNS=200). Depends on foreman-fe78.

title: Extract maxBudgetUsd values to environment variables
description: Now that foreman-fe78 landed maxBudgetUsd in roles.ts (explorer=$1.00, developer=$5.00, qa=$3.00, reviewer=$2.00) and dispatcher.ts (plan-step=$3.00), extract these to env vars for runtime tuning without code changes. Use FOREMAN_ prefix: FOREMAN_EXPLORER_BUDGET_USD, FOREMAN_DEVELOPER_BUDGET_USD, etc. Fall back to current hardcoded defaults when not set.

title: Compact scrollable agent status view with vim-style navigation
description: foreman status output is too verbose when running multiple agents — scrolls off the page. Two improvements: (1) Compact the per-agent display — collapse progress details into a single dense line (e.g. badge + bead + phase + stats on one line, cost on same line). (2) Add interactive scrollable mode with j/k (down/up) vim-style keybindings for navigating when agent list exceeds terminal height. Consider using blessed or ink for the TUI, or a simple raw-mode readline approach.
closeReason: Superseded by foreman-f18c which covers compact view + expandable detail toggle

title: Interactive expandable agent status with summary/detail toggle
description: Replace current flat agent status with an interactive list view. Summary row per agent: badge | bead-id | phase | elapsed | cost (one line). Pressing Enter on a selected agent expands to show full details (turns, tool calls, files changed, cost breakdown, last tool, sub-agents spawned). Enter again collapses back to summary. Navigate with j/k. This gives a clean overview when many agents are running while allowing drill-down into any specific agent.

title: Unify agent status display between status and run commands
description: The status command (src/cli/commands/status.ts) and run command have separate agent display code. After foreman-f18c lands the interactive expandable view, refactor to share a single status rendering module used by both commands. Extract the agent display logic into a shared component (e.g. src/cli/components/agent-status.ts) that both commands consume.

title: Add destructive command guard for agent workers
description: Agents run with bypassPermissions — add a guardrail that blocks destructive commands (rm -rf, git reset --hard, git push --force, DROP TABLE, etc.) before they reach the shell. Inspired by Flywheel's DCG. Implement as a command filter in agent-worker.ts that intercepts Bash tool calls and rejects dangerous patterns. Should be configurable via allowlist for legitimate use cases.
closeReason: Superseded by foreman-6e4d — integrate DCG instead of building our own

title: Add pre-commit bug scanning to finalize phase
description: Run pattern-based bug scanning before the finalize phase commits code. Inspired by Flywheel's UBS (1000+ rules). Start with a focused ruleset: console.log left in production code, TODO/FIXME/HACK markers, hardcoded secrets patterns, common security antipatterns (eval, innerHTML, SQL string concat). Fail finalize if critical patterns found, warn on others.

title: Cross-session memory for agent learning
description: Agents should learn from past runs on the same codebase. Inspired by Flywheel's CASS Memory three-layer architecture. Store per-project knowledge: (1) episodic — what happened in past runs (failures, successes, patterns), (2) working — current session context, (3) procedural — learned skills/patterns (e.g. 'this project needs npm run build before tests'). Surface relevant memories in TASK.md or explorer prompts.
closeReason: Superseded by foreman-1b30 — integrate CASS Memory instead of building our own

title: PageRank-based task prioritization for sd ready
description: Auto-prioritize tasks based on dependency graph centrality rather than manual P0-P4 assignment. Inspired by Flywheel's Beads Viewer. Tasks that unblock the most downstream work should bubble to top of sd ready. Could be implemented as a foreman layer on top of sd graph data, computing PageRank scores and sorting ready tasks by score.

title: Agent messaging system for flexible multi-agent coordination
description: Replace report files with a structured messaging system for inter-agent communication. Inspired by Flywheel's MCP Agent Mail. Agents send typed messages (report, question, handoff, feedback) with metadata. Messages are persisted and searchable. Enables more flexible workflows than fixed report files — e.g. developer asking explorer for clarification mid-implementation.
closeReason: Superseded by foreman-e8e3 — integrate MCP Agent Mail instead of building our own

title: Skill mining from past agent sessions
description: Automatically extract reusable patterns from successful past runs. Inspired by Flywheel's Meta Skill. After a pipeline completes successfully, analyze what worked: build commands, test patterns, common file structures, effective prompts. Store as project-specific skills that get injected into future agent prompts. Progressive improvement over time.

title: Multi-repo orchestration support
description: Support dispatching agents across multiple related repositories (e.g. frontend + backend + shared libs). Inspired by Flywheel's Repo Updater. foreman run should be able to target tasks that span repos, creating worktrees in each and coordinating changes. Lower priority — monorepo support covers most cases.

title: Integrate DCG (Destructive Command Guard) into foreman agent workers
description: Install and configure Dicklesworthstone/destructive_command_guard as a pre-execution hook for agent workers. DCG is a standalone Rust binary — install via curl, configure via ~/.config/dcg/config.toml. Steps: (1) Add DCG install check to foreman init, (2) Configure DCG TOML with foreman-specific allowlist, (3) Wire as pre-execution hook in agent-worker spawn env, (4) Log DCG blocks in run progress. Replaces foreman-48f8 (build our own guard). DCG covers git, shell, cloud, k8s, docker with 49+ security packs.

title: Integrate MCP Agent Mail for inter-agent communication
description: Replace report files (EXPLORER_REPORT.md, etc.) with MCP Agent Mail (Dicklesworthstone/mcp_agent_mail) for structured async messaging between pipeline phases. Agent Mail is a Python FastMCP HTTP server. Steps: (1) Add agent-mail server lifecycle to foreman run (start/stop), (2) Register each phase agent with unique identity, (3) Replace report file writes with mail thread messages (thread ID = bead ID), (4) Use file leasing for worktree conflict prevention in multi-agent scenarios, (5) Preserve report file generation as fallback/export. Replaces foreman-3a5d (build our own messaging).

title: Integrate CASS Memory System for cross-session agent learning
description: Integrate Dicklesworthstone/cass_memory_system for persistent agent learning across runs. CASS Memory is a TypeScript/Bun MCP server with three-layer architecture: episodic (raw session logs), working (structured summaries), procedural (confidence-tracked rules). Steps: (1) Feed foreman run logs into CASS episodic layer, (2) Run reflection after pipeline completion to update working/procedural memory, (3) Inject relevant memories via 'cm context <task> --json' into TASK.md before agent dispatch, (4) Parse agent feedback comments for memory reinforcement. Replaces foreman-7e84 (build our own memory). Depends on having enough session history.

title: Event-driven status refresh via agent worker notifications
description: Replace polling in foreman status --watch with event-driven updates. Agent workers already flush progress to SQLite every 2s. Add a notification mechanism (Unix socket or sentinel file) so workers signal the status process on state changes (phase transitions, completion, errors). Status process watches for signals and only re-renders when something changes. Reduces unnecessary sd CLI calls and gives instant feedback on phase transitions.

title: Detect and fix seed/agent state mismatches in foreman reset
description: When a run gets stuck or fails before markStuck() can reset the seed, the seed stays in_progress with no running agent. foreman reset should detect this mismatch: find all seeds with status=in_progress, check if they have a corresponding running agent in SQLite, and reset orphaned seeds back to open. Also add this check to foreman status as a warning (e.g. '1 orphaned task — run foreman reset').

title: Merge queue
description: Epic for improving worktree and merge handling, inspired by Overstory's approach. Covers AI conflict resolution, persistent merge queue, conflict learning, worktree CLI, safe branch deletion, state file handling, and more.

title: AI-powered conflict resolution
description: Add tiered AI conflict resolution before creating PRs. Tier 1: clean merge. Tier 2: auto-resolve (keep-incoming/union). Tier 3: send conflict markers to Claude for resolution (validate output isn't prose). Tier 4: abort merge, get both versions, ask Claude to reimplement changes. Currently Foreman jumps straight to PR creation on any code conflict.

title: Auto-commit state files before merge
description: Auto-commit .seeds/ and .foreman/ changes before merge attempts so uncommitted state files don't cause merge failures. Overstory auto-commits a known list of state file paths before attempting any merge. Foreman currently only stashes.

title: Persistent FIFO merge queue
description: Add a SQLite-backed persistent merge queue so completed agent work is queued for merge across CLI invocations and concurrent agents. Track status (pending/merging/merged/conflict/failed) and resolved tier. Use WAL mode for concurrent access. Currently merge is inline in the refinery — if the process dies mid-merge, state is lost.

title: Safe branch deletion (merge-status aware)
description: Check if a branch is actually merged before deleting it using git merge-base --is-ancestor. Only force-delete when explicitly requested. Currently Foreman always uses git branch -D which risks losing unmerged work.

title: Dedicated worktree management commands
description: Add foreman worktree list [--json] and foreman worktree clean [--completed|--all] [--force] commands. Currently worktree cleanup is buried in doctor --fix and reset. These should be first-class commands for inspecting and cleaning up worktrees.

title: Seeds preservation for non-merged branches
description: Preserve seed/task status changes from agent branches even if the branch itself isn't merged. Use three-dot diff to extract .seeds/ changes, write to temp patch, apply to canonical branch. Important for multi-agent orchestration where lead/coordinator branches are never merged but do update task state.

title: Conflict pattern learning
description: Record merge conflict outcomes (files, tier, success/failure) and use history to skip resolution strategies that historically fail for similar file patterns. Skip tiers with >=2 failures and 0 successes for overlapping files. Provide past successful resolutions as context to AI-resolve tier. Builds on AI conflict resolution story.

title: Merge dry-run mode
description: Add foreman merge --dry-run to preview what would be merged without executing. Show branch names, file changes, potential conflicts. Currently merge --list shows pending work but doesn't simulate the merge.

title: Untracked file conflict prevention
description: Detect untracked files that would conflict with an incoming merge and handle them (delete with warnings or stash) rather than failing with a git error. Currently Foreman would hit a git error and abort.

title: Merge queue health checks in doctor
description: Extend foreman doctor to detect stale merge queue entries (pending/merging >24h) and duplicate branches in the queue. Auto-fix by deleting stale entries and deduplicating. Builds on persistent merge queue story.
